seoTitle: How to Send Power BI Reports in Email Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to set up email subscriptions in Power BI Service to automatically distribute report snapshots to your team on a schedule.
intro: Manually exporting PDFs every morning to share with your executive team isn't a scalable habit. This guide shows how to use Power BI's native subscription feature to automatically deliver report snapshots straight to inboxes on a recurring schedule. You'll learn how to manage recipients, customize the message, and choose between embedded previews or full attachments.

steps.heading: Open the subscription menu
steps.body: In Power BI Service, open your target report and click the Subscribe to report icon in the top menu strip.

steps.heading: Add a new subscription
steps.body: Click Add new subscription, then enter recipient email addresses in the user field.

steps.heading: Customize the message
steps.body: Write a clear subject line and a message body describing the core metrics for your audience.

steps.heading: Choose format and schedule
steps.body: Select whether to embed the report as an image or attach it, then set the frequency to daily, weekly, or after data refresh, and pick your time zone.

steps.heading: Test and save
steps.body: Click Send now to run an immediate delivery test, review the email formatting, then save and close the subscription.

faq.question: Can I schedule Power BI email reports to send automatically after each data refresh?
faq.answer: Yes, the schedule settings let you trigger delivery after data refresh instead of a fixed time if you prefer always-current data.

faq.question: What formats can I send in a report subscription?
faq.answer: You can send embedded visual page previews as images or append full PDF or PowerPoint attachments.

faq.question: Can I add a security group as a recipient?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add individual team members or entire active directory security groups to a single subscription.

faq.question: How do I test my subscription before it goes live on schedule?
faq.answer: Use the Send now utility button to trigger an immediate test delivery and check the formatting in your inbox.
